# get_raster_values { #geost.spatial.get_raster_values }

```python
spatial.get_raster_values(x, y, raster_to_read)
```

Return sampled values from a raster at the given (x, y) locations.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name           | Type                        | Description                                                                                                                                  | Default    |
|----------------|-----------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| x              | np.ndarray                  | 1D array of x-coordinates, same length as 'y'.                                                                                               | _required_ |
| y              | np.ndarray                  | 1D array of y-coordinates, same length as 'x'.                                                                                               | _required_ |
| raster_to_read | str \| Path \| xr.DataArray | Location of a raster file or an xr.DataArray with dimensions 'x' and 'y'. This raster is used to sample values from at all (x, y) locations. | _required_ |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type       | Description                |
|--------|------------|----------------------------|
|        | np.ndarray | 1D array of sampled values |
